# 'branch restack' with spice.restack.method=merge updates a branch
# by merging its new base into it, recording a merge commit
# instead of rebasing.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-27T18:24:42Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# create a feature branch that touches a different file than trunk,
# so the later restack does not conflict.
cp $WORK/extra/feature.txt feature.txt
git add feature.txt
gs bc -m feature

# go back to trunk and advance it with a non-conflicting change.
gs trunk
cp $WORK/extra/init.new.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Change init'

gs up
stderr 'feature: needs to be restacked'

# restack with the merge method.
git config spice.restack.method merge
gs branch restack

# the branch now has a merge commit with two parents:
# its original commit and the advanced trunk.
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph.txt

# the recorded stack state is consistent.
gs ll -a
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ll.txt

# both files are present on the restacked branch.
cmp feature.txt $WORK/extra/feature.txt
cmp init.txt $WORK/extra/init.new.txt

-- repo/init.txt --
initial init

-- extra/init.new.txt --
changed init

-- extra/feature.txt --
feature contents

-- golden/graph.txt --
*   5307948 (HEAD -> feature) Merge branch 'main' into feature
|\  
| * 57ab3b0 (main) Change init
* | 19872d3 feature
|/  
* d692027 Initial commit
-- golden/ll.txt --
┏━■ feature ◀
┃   5307948 Merge branch 'main' into feature (now)
┃   19872d3 feature (now)
main
